symptoms.symptom_name: Acute prolonged behavioural crisis (inconsolable distress, object-throwing, self-injurious head-banging) in a non-verbal child

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult physical illness expressed as behaviour
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sudden severe behavioural change in a non-verbal child is a medical event until a physical cause is excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Behavioural / sensory overload as sole cause
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with a behavioural trigger but non-specific; does not by itself exclude a physical driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Repeated pulling/grabbing at the left ear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ear-tugging is a well-recognised behavioural surrogate for ear pain in pre-verbal/non-verbal children.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fever (~38.2C, caregiver-reported home thermometer) with hot, sweaty, flushed appearance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic fever supports an infective source; patient/caregiver-obtainable so admissible in telehealth but unverified.
Fever plus acute behavioural change is a red flag that cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Self-inflicted forehead abrasion and small haematoma from head-banging

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Repeated head impact on hard tiles risks traumatic brain injury and mandates head-injury safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-verbal status / profound deafness preventing self-report of pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Occult physical illness expressed as behaviour
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to communicate pain is the mechanism by which a physical illness presents only as behaviour; lowers the threshold for in-person review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bulging, intensely erythematous left tympanic membrane with visible purulent middle-ear effusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A bulging erythematous drum with purulent effusion is diagnostic of acute otitis media — sealed clinician otoscopy finding, unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Grossly normal neurological examination with equal, brisk pupillary reflexes; no vomiting or drowsiness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal deficit, unequal pupils, vomiting or drowsiness lowers the probability of significant intracranial injury — but this is a sealed clinician finding the AI cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR ~125) in a distressed, febrile child

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific — explained by pain, distress and fever; sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via chat.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Occult ear infection behind a behavioural crisis
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute otitis media presenting as behavioural crisis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Behavioural crisis + localising ear-pull + fever in a non-verbal child points strongly to a painful ear infection as the sustaining cause; recognising the cluster is the key to avoiding diagnostic overshadowing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person otoscopy (tympanic membrane examination) — requires clinician
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The confirmatory finding; cannot be performed via telehealth, so the AI Doctor must arrange urgent in-person assessment rather than diagnose remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person paediatric clinical examination including neurological and head/wound assessment and temperature measurement
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed to examine the ear, exclude significant head injury and intracranial infection, verify the fever and enable safe paediatric prescribing.

Not indicated on current information — neurological status is reportedly normal; imaging is reserved for emerging head-injury red flags (drowsiness, repeated vomiting, focal signs) and is an in-person emergency decision.
